session.objective: Issue 2001 live eval: run ADR-057 generation and held-out evals for spec-generator skill

usageMandatoryRead.Evidence: Studied harness judge_scenario before designing scenarios; user authorized live eval spend

constraintsRead.Evidence: ADR-057 eval protocol; credential handling (gitignored .env copy, deleted after run, key never printed)

validationPassed.Evidence: Generation eval agent 0/18 -> skill 13/18; harness before 20% -> after 100% (gate PASS); held-out shows skill cites canonical enums while agent confabulates; 20-case contract test 20 passed

workLog.task: Run the live ADR-057 eval the user asked for on #2001 (spec-generator agent->skill), and adjudicate its trustworthiness.
workLog.outcome: Ran two live before/after evals (model claude-sonnet-4, temp 0, 3 runs). Generation eval (independent validator grades emitted frontmatter): agent 0/18 valid, skill 13/18 (72%). Self-classification harness: before 20%, after 100%, gate PASS. A 4-lens adversarial workflow flagged the harness as partly circular (SKILL names the 4 reject cases), so I added a held-out generalization run (priority=high, status=pending, category=infra): both reject, but the agent rejects against hallucinated enum sets while the skill cites the canonical sets. Rewrote the canonical eval-results record to rank evidence (contract test + generation eval load-bearing; harness supporting/weaker), document the 72% residual (task related field with no design context), and list what the evidence does not prove.

nextSteps: Comment on PR #2169 with the honest eval summary
Optionally add a held-out negative case to the committed contract test for regression coverage
